refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by ensuring timely data acquisition, processing, and dissemination.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, data processing, and data dissemination, which collectively impact end-to-end latency.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure latency at each sub-function stage (data acquisition, processing, dissemination) and end-to-end update latency under representative operational scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model rapidly
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to consumers within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with priority scheduling
DP2: Implement incremental model update algorithms to reduce processing time
DP3: Employ low-latency network protocols and efficient data serialization for dissemination
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to address acquisition, processing, and dissemination latency respectively

potential_sources: Tight coupling between data acquisition and processing timing
Dependency of dissemination on processing output format
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with well-defined interfaces to minimize cross-domain impact.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable high-frequency telemetry data
Risk of network congestion impacting dissemination latency
Processing algorithms must be optimized for worst-case scenarios
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor real-world telemetry and network performance to refine DPs and consider fallback mechanisms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating telemetry acquisition, model processing, and dissemination

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency data streams from vehicles and sensors

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to consumers and other modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes update logic and data flow, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source; limited containment of update logic.
Consumes updated model but does not perform update processing.
Uses safety alerts, not involved in update processing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module optimizes latency control and reduces cross-module dependencies.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Risk of single point of failure impacting update latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution to distribute load and improve fault tolerance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and mapped to logical DPs that address each latency-critical function.

diffusion_risks: Some coupling between acquisition and processing DPs may cause diffusion if not carefully modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning all update latency functions to NGHE Solution module provides strong containment and clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end and at sub-function boundaries within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update latency responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor real-world telemetry and network performance to validate assumptions.
Plan for modular subcomponents within NGHE Solution to mitigate bottlenecks.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment sufficiently addresses the latency requirement with clear ownership and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model rapidly
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to consumers within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Reliable high-frequency telemetry data is available.
Processing algorithms can meet worst-case latency targets.
open_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module.
Single point of failure risk in centralized update logic.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data frequency and reliability metrics.
Performance benchmarks of update processing algorithms.
